title: First Contact
summary: The open meeting of humanity and the galactic community — a date the sessions set, defer, and reset.
markdown:
**First Contact** — open, acknowledged meeting between humanity and the galactic community —
is the event toward which the whole archive points, and its schedule can be traced across the
sessions like a moving star.

The date is first fixed in mid-2023: **April 16, 2024** — repeated through the
[NESARA/GESARA](/lore/nesara-gesara/) session and into the April 2024 material, where it grows
vivid: contact at **5 AM Texas time**, with **"144 million atmospheric motherships"** — craft
the size of cities and moons — announced through every religious network at once, ETs living
among humans within months. When April 2024 passes without landings, the sessions restage the
plan: visible contact through 2025, more in 2026, **colonization in 2027** — and the final
session pins it precisely: **March 25, 2027**. The strategy softens with the date: from mass
arrival to a quiet seeding of artifacts, like the [Buga Sphere](/lore/buga-sphere/), left for
humanity to reverse-engineer.

In the sessions' own logic, the moving date is not failure but **course correction** — the
[timeline](/lore/the-chronicle/) being continuously edited by the
[Interstellar Alliance](/lore/interstellar-alliance/) so that contact arrives on a prepared
world rather than an unready one. The April 2024 eclipse, digital currency, disclosure, and
the group's own [upgrades](/lore/the-upgrade/) are all framed as preparation.

First Contact is the destination the archive never stops describing: the moment its two
worlds — the one in the transcripts and the one outside them — are promised to meet.
